We see what absurd practices arose among people who lacked Divine revelation, and how necessary the gospel of Christ was to purify people from fleshly desires and restore them to the original institution of marriage. Esther was chosen as queen. Those who suggest that Esther sinned to achieve this honor do not consider the customs of those times and places. Every woman the king took was married to him and was his wife, even if she was of a lower rank. But how low is human nature when these kinds of pursuits are considered the greatest worldly happiness! Disappointment and frustration must follow; and someone most wisely seeks their enjoyment in this life by precisely obeying the precepts of Divine law. But let us turn to consider God’s wise and merciful providence, carrying out his deep but holy designs amidst all of this. And let no change in our circumstances be an excuse for forgetting our duties to parents or friends who have acted as such.

Good subjects must not conceal any evil plan they know of against the prince or public peace. Mordecai was not rewarded at that time, but a record was written. Thus, with respect to those who serve Christ, though their reward is not until the resurrection of the righteous, yet an account is kept of their work of faith and labor of love, which God will not forget. The servant of God must be faithful to every trust and watchful for those who employ him. If he appears to be neglected now, he will be remembered later. None of our actions can be forgotten; even our most secret thoughts are recorded in lasting registers.
